headline_marked: [[Bangladesh unveils largest-ever budget]] of 9.38 lakh crore Taka for FY27
headline: Bangladesh unveils largest-ever budget of 9.38 lakh crore Taka for FY27
summary: The BNP-led government presents its first national budget, the biggest in Bangladesh's history, with a proposed outlay of 9.38 lakh crore Taka for the fiscal year 2026-27.
details: Finance Minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury presented the national budget for the fiscal year 2026-27 in Parliament today, marking the first budget under the leadership of the Tareq Rahman-led BNP government.
The proposed budget, with an estimated size of 9.38 lakh crore Taka, is the largest in the history of Bangladesh. This marks the 55th budget for the nation.
The budget includes proposals for the development of the Qawmi education sector, aiming to enhance educational opportunities and standards within this specific stream of education.
caption:
Bangladesh's largest-ever budget of 9.38 lakh crore Taka presented today.
This marks the first budget of the Tareq Rahman-led BNP government.

Finance Minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury presented the national budget for the fiscal year 2026-27 in Parliament today, marking the first budget under the leadership of the Tareq Rahman-led BNP government.

The proposed budget, with an estimated size of 9.38 lakh crore Taka, is the largest in the history of Bangladesh. This marks the 55th budget for the nation.

The budget includes proposals for the development of the Qawmi education sector, aiming to enhance educational opportunities and standards within this specific stream of education.

#BangladeshBudget #FY27 #BNP #AmirKhosru #NationalBudget

Source: Jamuna TV, Dhaka Tribune, Somoy News, Prothom Alo, The Daily Star
tweet: Bangladesh unveils its largest-ever budget of 9.38 lakh crore Taka for FY27. This is the first budget presented by the Tareq Rahman-led BNP government. #BangladeshBudget #FY27
source: Jamuna TV, Dhaka Tribune, Somoy News, Prothom Alo, The Daily Star

headline_marked: [[South Africa brace]] for Azteca test against Mexico in WC opener
headline: South Africa brace for Azteca test against Mexico in WC opener
summary: South Africa coach Hugo Broos challenges his players to block out the expected wall of sound at the Estadio Azteca. Mexico aims to break their winless streak in World Cup opening matches.
details: South Africa's coach Hugo Broos has urged his team to concentrate on their game despite an anticipated deafening crowd noise at the Estadio Azteca for their World Cup opener against co-hosts Mexico. Broos, who played at the Azteca in 1986, considers this match pivotal for South Africa's progression.

Broos anticipates an overwhelming atmosphere with "85,000 Mexicans shouting and singing," but stresses the importance of focus to perform well. He acknowledged Mexico's strong recent form, calling them "the best team in the group" and predicting a "very tough game" where South Africa must be "at their best level."

The expanded 48-team World Cup format has drawn criticism from Broos due to the demanding travel schedule, which includes games in Atlanta and Monterrey. He finds the current format "more exhausting" than previous World Cups.

Mexico, despite playing in seven World Cup opening matches, has never secured a victory. Coach Javier Aguirre is determined to change this statistic, stating, "We have to break the statistic" and plans to use it as motivation for his players.

Aguirre, who also played at the Azteca in 1986, expressed his deep emotion about experiencing a World Cup on home soil again after 40 years. He emphasized that hosting the tournament again is a significant moment for him and the team.
caption:
South Africa faces a daunting World Cup opener against co-hosts Mexico at the Estadio Azteca. Coach Hugo Broos challenges his players to overcome the expected 85,000-strong crowd noise.

South Africa's coach Hugo Broos has urged his team to concentrate on their game despite an anticipated deafening crowd noise at the Estadio Azteca for their World Cup opener against co-hosts Mexico. Broos, who played at the Azteca in 1986, considers this match pivotal for South Africa's progression.

Broos anticipates an overwhelming atmosphere with "85,000 Mexicans shouting and singing," but stresses the importance of focus to perform well. He acknowledged Mexico's strong recent form, calling them "the best team in the group" and predicting a "very tough game" where South Africa must be "at their best level."

The expanded 48-team World Cup format has drawn criticism from Broos due to the demanding travel schedule, which includes games in Atlanta and Monterrey. He finds the current format "more exhausting" than previous World Cups.

Mexico, despite playing in seven World Cup opening matches, has never secured a victory. Coach Javier Aguirre is determined to change this statistic, stating, "We have to break the statistic" and plans to use it as motivation for his players.

Aguirre, who also played at the Azteca in 1986, expressed his deep emotion about experiencing a World Cup on home soil again after 40 years. He emphasized that hosting the tournament again is a significant moment for him and the team.

#FIFAWorldCup #WorldCup2026 #SouthAfrica #Mexico #Football

Source: Somoy News, Jamuna TV, Prothom Alo, The Daily Star
tweet: South Africa's coach Hugo Broos urges focus against Mexico at the Estadio Azteca. "We have to focus on our game... if we can do that, if we are not too influenced by the noise of 85,000 Mexicans, then we can have a good game." #WorldCup2026 #SouthAfrica #Mexico
source: Somoy News, Jamuna TV, Prothom Alo, The Daily Star
